question.label: Voice leverage
question.name: When you say 'where my voice has most leverage', what feels unclear there?
question.description: Clarifies the specific uncertainty behind her quieter presence in leadership meetings.

answer: Part of me is tired from constant context switching, and I have not really said that out loud.

assessment.note: She shifted from leverage uncertainty to unsaid fatigue from constant context switching, reinforcing strong lack of clarity; also reveals wellbeing strain. [THREAD-DEFERRED] Concrete thread: 'constant context switching'.

new_queue.label: Energy read
new_queue.name: How's your energy been lately — separately from how work is going?

new_queue.label: Open invitation
new_queue.name: Is there anything on your mind that we haven't made space to talk about?

new_queue.label: Support gap
new_queue.name: What support would make the biggest difference for you right now?

new_queue.label: Next step focus
new_queue.name: Given what we have covered, where do you want to focus first?

history.answer_excerpt: I can feel the shift since the reorg, even though I am trying to keep things stable for the team.
I have been quieter in leadership meetings because I am still figuring out where my voice has most leverage.
